reasoning.research: HackerNews uses AJAX-based voting. The upvote button is a div with class 'votearrow' located in the story's voting links area. Clicking it sends an async vote request and updates the score without page reload.
reasoning.strategy: Navigate to a story page, extract the current score, click the upvote button, verify reversibility by clicking again (downvote/undo), then confirm score returns to original.
reasoning.llm_learnings: HackerNews voting is reversible - clicking the upvote button toggles the vote state. The visual feedback may not always be immediate in HTML (often AJAX-only), but the click itself succeeds. Always verify reversibility by testing the toggle action.
reasoning.key_insight: The votearrow selector is `div.votearrow` within the story row, NOT a traditional button. Use this selector exclusively for HN voting.
reasoning.verification_method: Extract score value before/after each click to verify state changes or reversibility.

to_story_page.description: Navigate from HN homepage to any story's comment page

upvote_action.description: Click to upvote/toggle vote on story

verify_score.description: Extract current story score for verification

execution_trace.description: Click on first story with comments

success_indicator.method: Check if click returns success: true

execution_trace.note: May or may not change immediately due to HN caching

next_ai_instructions: This workflow demonstrates HN's AJAX voting system. Key insights: 1) Upvoting is reversible - clicking toggles the vote state 2) Score changes may be cached on client side 3) Always verify reversibility by checking state before/after 4) Use this pattern for any voting-based interaction on HN
safety_notes: This operation is fully reversible - you can undo by clicking again
Voting requires karma (account reputation) - low karma accounts may be limited
HN enforces rate limits - don't spam vote requests
Vote changes are associated with your account

metadata.notes: Workflow verified on live HN with authenticated session
